body_txt: A very messed-up theory that's a variant of typical karma . The basic premise of karma is: "Don't do something bad, because later on something bad will happen to you ." This, on the other hand, is quite different, and can best be summed up as an alteration of the above: "It's okay if someone does something bad to you, because later on you can do something bad to someone else. " A scenario: a man running a company is under a great deal of pressure from his superiors to produce the product faster. He's just been yelled at. Obviously, he can't talk back to his supervisor, since he'd get fired, so what does he do? He chews out his normally best worker over something small. Well, the target man goes home and yells at his wife, to displace all the anger that he can't vent at work. Because of this marital strife, she screams at their kid all the time. The kid, having no weaker human to take it out on, beats the family dog, which then bites the nearby cat, and so on. The strong attacks the weak, who turns around and gives similar treatment to the weaker, who then harms the next weakest in turn. And so on and so forth, with today's victim becoming tomorrow's victimizer seemingly without end. That's only "fair", right? Inspired by a dynamic that's all too often Truth in Television , this scenario may have some additional elements: The act of abusing someone weaker or lower-ranking than you to displace frustrations because of similar treatment from someone stronger or higher-ranking than you is often known as Kicking the Dog , but that term has a different meaning on this wiki. Contrast with Paying It Forward : the "chain of help". Also contrast The Forgiven Offer Forgiveness , which is an inversion. Contrast with Cycle of Revenge , which directly targets the person causing harm. Compare Revenge by Proxy , Toxic Family Influence , Delegation Relay , and Troubled Abuser . Breaking the Cycle of Bad Parenting is an effort to avert or defy this. If you're looking for examples of people using literal chains to harm others, see Chain Pain . See also Create Your Own Villain , when one or more of the victims goes bad and hurts others as a natural consequence, and Then Let Me Be Evil , when a character who's good at best (or slightly evil at worst) gives in and becomes genuinely evil because of the way they've been treated by everyone else. Can sometimes be a form of Spreading the Misery . Contrast with The Dog Bites Back , when the abused will give the abuser their Just Desserts , and He Who Fights Monsters , when said fighting back against other abusers devolves into hurting other people in the process. See Freudian Excuse when this is used in a villain's backstory as an explanation for their behavior now. Sometimes played as An Aesop (e.g., "Don't be mean to someone because someone was mean to you, or it will go on forever"). See also Generational Trauma .
